act_name_th: พระราชบัญญัติอาคารชุด
amendments: As amended by Condominium Act (No. 4) B.E. 2551 (2008)

17.text_en: Section 17. An apartment owner shall have a right of co-ownership in common property proportionally to the ratio of the area of the apartment owned to the total area of all apartments in such condominium, unless the by-laws provide otherwise.

18.text_en: Section 18. The apartment owner shall have a right in the common property and may not demand partition of the common property.

19.text_en: Section 19. Aliens or juristic persons as specified in Section 97 and Section 98 of the Land Code who are qualified under Section 19 bis may own apartments in the condominium.

19 bis.amendment_notes: Added by Condominium Act (No. 4) B.E. 2551 (2008). Previously Section 19 contained this content.

19 ter.text_en:
Section 19 ter. The ratio of floor space of all apartments in the condominium owned by persons under Section 19 bis shall altogether not exceed forty-nine percent of the total floor space of all apartments in such condominium at the time of the registration of the condominium under Section 6.
If the condominium is near any boundary of the Kingdom or in any area that affects national security, the ratio of floor space under paragraph one may be prescribed by the Minister in a Ministerial Regulation to be less than the rate prescribed in paragraph one.
If the apartment ownership ratio of persons under Section 19 bis reaches the limit prescribed in paragraph one or paragraph two, the registration of apartment ownership for persons under Section 19 bis shall not be accepted.

19 quater.text_en: Section 19 quater. A juristic person under Section 19 bis (5) shall produce evidence of bringing in foreign currency into the Kingdom or evidence of withdrawal of money from a Thai Baht account of persons having residence outside the Kingdom or evidence of withdrawal of money from a foreign currency account in an amount not less than the price of the apartment to be purchased.

19 quinque.text_en:
Section 19 quinque. Any person who acquired ownership of an apartment while being of Thai nationality and subsequently lost Thai nationality or whose Thai nationality has been revoked shall give notification to the competent official within sixty days from the date of loss of Thai nationality and shall dispose of all apartments owned within one year from the date of loss of Thai nationality.
If the apartment is not disposed of within the time prescribed, the provisions of Section 19 octo shall apply mutatis mutandis.

19 sexto.text_en:
Section 19 sexto. If persons under Section 19 bis acquire ownership of an apartment by inheritance and such acquisition when added to that already owned exceeds the ratio prescribed in Section 19 ter, such person shall dispose of the apartment in the portion that exceeds the prescribed ratio within one year from the date of acquisition.
If the apartment is not disposed of within the time prescribed, the provisions of Section 19 octo shall apply mutatis mutandis.

19 octo.text_en: Section 19 octo. If the apartment is not disposed of within the time prescribed in Section 19 quinque or Section 19 sexto, the Director-General shall have the power to dispose of such apartment in accordance with the rules, procedure and conditions prescribed in the Ministerial Regulation. The proceeds from the disposal, after deducting expenses, shall be returned to the owner.
